selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and to employ strategies that can help identify and filter out false information. One approach is to use fact-checking tools and third-party verification services, such as Snopes or FactCheck.org, to verify the accuracy of content before sharing it. Additionally, social media platforms themselves have implemented various measures to detect and remove fake content, such as identifying and flagging suspicious accounts for review. However, it is important to remember that no solution can completely eliminate fake content, so it is also essential to cultivate a critical and discerning mindset when consuming information online.",
"reasoning_summary": "To identify and filter out fake content on social media, one should employ strategies such as using fact-checking tools and third-party verification services, and cultivating a critical and discerning mindset. Social media platforms have also implemented measures to detect and remove fake content.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [
"Snopes. (n.d.). Fact Checking. Retrieved from <https://www.snopes.com/fact-checking/>"
],
"safety_notes": "",
"used_persona_fields": ["persona"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be aware of the tactics used by fake accounts and to employ strategies that can help identify and filter out false information. One approach is to use fact-checking tools and third-party verification services, such as Snopes or FactCheck.org, to verify the accuracy of content before sharing it. Additionally, social media platforms themselves have implemented various measures to detect and remove fake content, such as identifying and flagging suspicious accounts for review. However, it is important to remember that no solution can completely eliminate fake content, so it is also essential to cultivate a critical and discerning mindset when consuming information online.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To identify and filter out fake content on social media, one should employ strategies such as using fact-checking tools and third-party verification services, and cultivating a critical and discerning mindset. Social media platforms have also implemented measures to detect and remove fake content.
